paragraphs.text: God, who made all things by His word out of nothing, did not commit this to writing at the beginning, because the beginning of all things was open to the knowledge of Adam. And the generations nearest to him learned this from their forefathers. But when all had turned aside from God and were in ignorance of all the works of God, then God through Moses committed this to writing for the Hebrew people, because men had altered the nature that bears witness to the creation of all beings. Moses wrote in the wilderness what to Adam’s mind was open in paradise.

From the first peoples, who knew this without writings; from the people that received this in Scripture and believed; from the later peoples, who received Scripture from the people of God; and from those who still abide by their own sacrifices and have not believed — to God and Christ and the Holy Spirit be glory and honor, both now and at all times, and to the ages of ages! Amen and Amen.
